content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a modular backbone that integrates multiple inconsistent perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical reflection system that outperforms either standalone approach without requiring extra training or labeled data. This system first generates diverse conflicting candidate outputs from different solving perspectives, then runs iterative self-feedback refinement on each candidate to resolve inconsistencies, rather than only refining a single initial output.
